title: Tender Cheesy Uruguayan Beef Lasagna
description: A kid-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring garlicky beef and a cheese-pull effect, made with cocoa powder for added depth of flavor, this low-glycemic-friendly lasagna is a perfect dinner option
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Boil the lasagna noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the garlic powder, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Stir in the cocoa powder and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato sauce to the skillet and bring to a simmer, reducing the heat to low and letting it cook while you assemble the lasagna.
In a large bowl, combine the mozzarella and parmesan cheese.

Spread a layer of the meat sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish, followed by a layer of noodles, then a layer of the cheese mixture, repeating this process until all of the ingredients have been used, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
